This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] }

Close_Notes: Detailed description of issue: IDOC Failed in RTR Stream for Exchange rates 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A

Description_Text: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' Issue ID: 853f40d8-4dfc-4f54-8195-ffedefea8e5a Priority: Critical Alert Policies: - SAP_Core_IDoc_RTR_SNOW_Alert Impacted Entities: - Replication of Currency Rates Alert Conditions: - Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production Additional Issue Data Additional Attributes: N/A Additional Payload: { "alertConditionDescription": [ "Core RTR (Record to Report) IDoc failures detected in the Production environment. This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] }
Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A

Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A

Description_Text: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' Issue ID: 152b173f-7ba7-42cc-a1a5-ea5f83966eb7 Priority: Critical Alert Policies: - SAP_Core_IDoc_RTR_SNOW_Alert Impacted Entities: - Replication of Currency Rates Alert Conditions: - Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production Additional Issue Data Additional Attributes: N/A Additional Payload: { "alertConditionDescription": [ "Core RTR (Record to Report) IDoc failures detected in the Production environment. This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] }
Work_Notes: 2025-11-22 02:03:49 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) New Relic alert 152b173f-7ba7-42cc-a1a5-ea5f83966eb7 closed at 2025-11-22 02:03:41.
Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: IDOC Failed in RTR Stream for Exchange rates 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A

Description_Text: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' Issue ID: 91a9239e-483e-4c17-a75d-da67327c0781 Priority: Critical Alert Policies: - SAP_Core_IDoc_RTR_SNOW_Alert Impacted Entities: - Replication of Currency Rates Alert Conditions: - Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production Additional Issue Data Additional Attributes: N/A Additional Payload: { "alertConditionDescription": [ "Core RTR (Record to Report) IDoc failures detected in the Production environment. This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] }
Work_Notes: 2025-11-25 01:54:00 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) New Relic alert 91a9239e-483e-4c17-a75d-da67327c0781 closed at 2025-11-25 01:53:53.
Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: IDOC Failed in RTR Stream for Exchange rates 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A

Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: IDOC Failed in RTR Stream for Exchange rates 2. Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : Yes /No No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Change Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 8. Problem Request Number [If Applicable] : N/A 9. Sub-Category : IDOC failure 10. Automation / Elimination / Documentation Possible (Mention applicable one, if applicable): Yes. Documentation is possible

Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: IDOC Failed in RTR Stream for Exchange rates 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] } 2026-02-28 09:09:39 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) New Relic alert 8b5e8f17-ca3d-4773-b1cd-362cc6c5bc10 closed at 2026-02-28 09:09:30. 2026-02-27 09:09:24 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) [code] Update from New Relic [/code][code] Triggering Action[/code]: INCIDENT ADDED[code] [/code] [code] Total Incidents [/code]: 4 2026-02-27 08:53:59 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) [code] Update from New Relic [/code][code] Triggering Action[/code]: INCIDENT ADDED[code] [/code] [code] Total Incidents [/code]: 3 [code] Impacted Entities [/code]: ["Replication of Currency Rates","Bank statement"] 2026-02-27 02:03:54 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) [code] Update from New Relic [/code][code] Triggering Action[/code]: INCIDENT ADDED[code] [/code] [code] Total Incidents [/code]: 2 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: N/A 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Short_Description: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production'
Description_Text: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' Issue ID: 8472b3b5-6bcb-487e-8480-811eb3d75abf Priority: Critical Alert Policies: - SAP_Core_IDoc_RTR_SNOW_Alert Impacted Entities: - Replication of Currency Rates Alert Conditions: - Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production Additional Issue Data Additional Attributes: N/A Additional Payload: { "alertConditionDescription": [ "Core RTR (Record to Report) IDoc failures detected in the Production environment. This indicates that one or more IDocs have failed during processing.\nImmediate investigation is required to identify the root cause." ] }
Work_Notes: 2026-03-13 07:44:13 - S Suraksha (Work notes) Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. We are rejecting this incident. 2026-03-12 02:04:08 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) New Relic alert 8472b3b5-6bcb-487e-8480-811eb3d75abf closed at 2026-03-12 02:03:58. 2026-03-12 02:04:01 - SVCNR USER (Work notes) [code] Update from New Relic [/code][code] Triggering Action[/code]: INCIDENT ADDED[code] [/code] [code] Total Incidents [/code]: 69 2026-03-11 09:49:39 - Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: Problem ticket has been raised and we are working on it 4. Workaround Applied (Give details if Yes) : No 5. Dependency with other modules : No Specify team name if Yes – 6. Customer Feedback/Verification (Yes/No): Yes 7. Automation / Elimination / Documentation Possible (Mention applicable one, if applicable): Yes. Documentation is possible

Close_Notes: 1. Detailed description of issue: New Relic Issue: Replication of Currency Rates query result is >= 1.0 on 'Core RTR IDoc failures detected in Production' 2. Root cause of service disruption: Core is receiving the exchange rate from Treasury which is EURX and it is internally converted into M. whereas other systems like Zycus and Pricefx are using the Exchange rate M hence IDOC are failing due to out of scope data (Exchange Rate Type M/ Currency pairs availability) in CORE which can be ignored. We had a discussion with PO and solace team earlier on this and understood that Solace team will send the payload file which is received from PO to all their related systems i.e., Zycus/Pricefx and CORE. so, we are receiving the Exchange rate type "M" which is not in scope for Rwanda/Serbia/Egypt hence we can see the IDOC failure. As of now, Rwanda/Egypt and Serbia are okay with current setup in CORE and failure IDOC can be ignored. 3. Action Taken: Problem ticket has been raised and we are working on it 4.
